assets.name: Code source integrity
assets.description: Source code matches the sealed manifest.

assets.name: Seal integrity
assets.description: GLOBAL_SEAL_HASH faithfully represents the source code state.

assets.name: Audit log immutability
assets.description: Once recorded, an audit log entry cannot be modified without invalidating the chain.

assets.name: Consensus robustness
assets.description: Consensus cannot produce ACT without a 3/4 supermajority.

assets.name: Formal proof validity
assets.description: Lean theorems are correct and relevant.

adversaries.name: Skeptical external auditor
adversaries.capabilities: Read-only access; can run all verification scripts.

adversaries.name: Corrupted consensus node operator
adversaries.capabilities: Full control of 1 or 2 nodes out of 4.

adversaries.name: Network attacker (MITM)
adversaries.capabilities: Can intercept and modify communications between nodes.

surfaces.name: Git repository
surfaces.description: Direct push, tag modification, history rewrite.

surfaces.name: Seal scripts
surfaces.description: generate_seal.py, seal_verify.py; manipulation of exclusions and hash rules.

assumptions.name: ED25519 unforgeability
assumptions.description: ED25519 signature algorithm is considered unforgeable.

assumptions.name: Execution environment integrity
assumptions.description: The OS and hardware are not compromised.

assumptions.name: GitHub integrity
assumptions.description: GitHub preserves the integrity of commits and tags once pushed.

claims.claim: Modification of a sealed file is detected by seal_verify.py.
Any change to a tracked file changes the ROOT_HASH.
A split consensus (2 ACT / 2 HOLD) results in BLOCK (fail-closed).
Modification of an audit log entry invalidates the hash chain.
Modification of a Merkle leaf changes the root (formally proved in Lean).
The decision engine is monotone (higher S never yields a weaker decision).
The decision engine is deterministic at the theta_S boundary.
Any change to ROOT_HASH changes the GLOBAL_SEAL_HASH.
